div.head:
No. 512
Editorial Note
div.p: During the Senate Foreign Relations Committee hearings on the Mutual Security Act of 1952, which began on March 13 and continued through April 4, former Ambassador in France David K. E. Bruce appeared before the committee to give testimony concerning the political and economic conditions in France. For the text of Bruce’s statement made before the Committee on March 25, see Department of State Bulletin, April 7, 1952, pages 533–534. Regarding these and other hearings relating to the Mutual Security Act of 1952, see Document 255.
